title: taraxacum gaditanum
intro: Taraxacum gaditanum, also known as the Canarian dandelion or Canarian hawk's beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It is a perennial herb native to the Canary Islands, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible leaves. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum gaditanum include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to support liver and digestive health. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support overall well-being, and it is often used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions.
